tech.vendor: DFM Associates (electronic pollbook; vote-center check-in connected to the county Election Information Management System / EIMS)
tech.note: CA SoS 'Voting Technologies in Use by County' (as of Oct 10, 2025) lists Fresno's Electronic Pollbook as 'DFM' (DFM Associates). Fresno's Election Administration Plan (Jan 14, 2022, https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/vca/eap/fresno-eap.pdf) describes vote-center technology: 'Vote Centers will be connected to the County's Election Information Management System (EIMS) to allow Vote Center staff to verify, in real time, the voting status of each voter,' and references a powered 'check-in station' at each vote center. Electronic pollbooks/check-in were first deployed when Fresno conducted its first all-mail/vote-center election, the March 3, 2020 Presidential Primary (53 vote centers). Like Madera, the e-pollbook function is DFM/EIMS-connected check-in rather than a dedicated tablet product (no KNOWiNK/Tenex tablet documented). Board of Supervisors adopted the Voter's Choice Act model on March 12, 2019; first VCA election March 2020.

ADOPTED. Fresno County uses automated signature-verification software for the FIRST-pass comparison of VBM envelope signatures. Per an Oct 12, 2020 op-ed by Brandi Orth, Fresno County Clerk/Registrar of Voters: 'The Fresno County Elections Office uses a signature verification software program that completes the first comparison. After this automated comparison, approximately 50% of the signatures are compared and verified'; signatures the software cannot verify are then 'viewed first-hand by staff trained to compare signatures.' Software vendor not named in the source (Fresno's voting system is Dominion ImageCast, purchased 2019). adopted_year/first_election set to the Nov 3, 2020 General, the election during which the Registrar publicly documented the automated software in use; the software may predate that (possibly introduced with the 2019 Dominion system / March 2020 VCA launch) but no earlier primary date was located. The EAP's generic line ('Signatures on every VBM ballot envelope are compared to the voter's registration signature and must match before being counted') does not by itself indicate automation; the op-ed is the primary evidence that the first pass is software-automated. VERIFIER (2026-06): op-ed re-opened and confirmed verbatim — Brandi Orth, Fresno County Clerk/Registrar of Voters, Oct 12, 2020: 'uses a signature verification software program that completes the first comparison' (~50% auto-verified, remainder hand-reviewed), so ASV is genuinely software-automated (status remains adopted; not humans-by-eye). However the SOLE evidence is a registrar-authored op-ed published on a news outlet (gvwire.com / GV Wire), not an official registrar/SoS/CalVoter document; confidence downgraded primary -> secondary per the source-type rule. adopted_year=2020 retained as a first-election year (the Nov 2020 General the op-ed documents), not a board-vote/contract date; an earlier first-use election was not located.

No in-vote-center mail-ballot signature-verify-and-scan ('Sign, Scan & Go', AB 626) capability described in Fresno's Election Administration Plan. Returned VBM ballots dropped at the 53 vote centers / 65 drop boxes are transported to the central Elections Office for signature verification and central tabulation (Dominion ICC/ICE); there is no mention of verifying and scanning returned VBM envelopes at the point of return inside a vote center. AB 626 sign-scan-go is associated with Placer County, not Fresno. Finding by absence of evidence in the primary EAP (secondary confidence).

Fresno adopted the California Voter's Choice Act all-mailed-ballot + vote-center model. The Board of Supervisors voted March 12, 2019 to adopt the VCA 'in time for the 2020 elections' (CA SoS news release); Fresno was the ninth county to implement the VCA. First election conducted under the model was the March 3, 2020 Presidential Primary, with 53 vote centers and drop boxes replacing 268 one-day precinct polling places (Fresno EAP, https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/vca/eap/fresno-eap.pdf). Board-vote/adoption date = March 12, 2019; first election conducted with the model = March 2020.

metrics.denominator: ballots counted to date (CalVoter basis: % of Nov 8, 2022 ballots reported as counted one week after Election Day)
metrics.note: chart-read. CalVoter 'Publicly Reported California Ballot Counting Progress on Nov 15, 2022' (Percentage of Nov 08, 2022 ballots reported as counted one week after Election Day; data as of 10:10 AM PT Nov 16, 2022). Fresno = 84.7%; statewide reference 77.7%. Cross-checks confirmed in the same chart: Madera 67.6%, San Mateo 83.2% (match prior records).

metrics.denominator: ballots counted to date (CalVoter basis: % of Nov 5, 2024 ballots counted one week after Election Day)
metrics.note: chart-read. CalVoter yellow chart, data as of 10:06 PM PT Nov 12, 2024 (% of Nov 2024 ballots counted one week after Election Day). Fresno = 81.9%; statewide reference 86.1%. Cross-checks in same chart: Madera 81.2%, San Mateo 83.4% (match prior records).

metrics.denominator: ballots counted to date (CalVoter basis: % of Nov. 4, 2025 ballots counted one week after Election Day)
metrics.note: Latest odd-year. chart-read. CalVoter 'Publicly Reported California Ballot Counting Progress on Nov 11, 2025' (Percentage of Nov. 4 ballots counted one week after Election Day). Fresno = 70.4%; statewide reference 93.9%. Cross-check in same chart: Madera 76.1% (matches prior record). Note: under SB 280, counties were prohibited from certifying results until at least 28 days after Election Day.

Not computed: would require Fresno's first post-poll-close (election-night) ballot total divided by the certified-final ballot total. The first-report ballot count paired with the certified-final denominator was not collected in this pass; not substituting a different denominator (e.g. % of registered voters). Value null per metric rules.
Fresno does not publish per-day/per-release canvass totals in a readily machine-readable form, so the day on which >=90% of the certified final was counted cannot be determined.

notes: Fresno County, CA. TECH: Adopted the California Voter's Choice Act (all-mail + vote-center model) by Board of Supervisors vote on March 12, 2019, first conducted at the March 3, 2020 Presidential Primary (the ninth CA county to adopt VCA; 53 vote centers replacing 268 one-day precincts). Electronic pollbooks date to that first VCA election (March 2020); Fresno's certified Electronic Pollbook is 'DFM' (DFM Associates) per CA SoS 'Voting Technologies in Use by County' (Oct 10, 2025), used as EIMS-connected real-time check-in at vote centers (same DFM/EIMS pattern as Madera, not a dedicated tablet product). Voting/tabulation system (context, not one of the four tracked types): Dominion ICC 5.10A / ICE 5.10A central+vote-center tabulation, ICX 5.10A BMDs, Dominion MBP, with Democracy Live Secure Select RAVBM (new $2.9M system purchased 2019). KEY FINDING: ASV is ADOPTED (not the typical CA 'humans-by-eye'): the Registrar's own Oct 2020 op-ed states Fresno uses signature-verification SOFTWARE for the first comparison (~50% auto-verified), with staff hand-reviewing the rest. Vendor not named; exact first-use year not pinned (documented Nov 2020). Sign-Scan-Go (AB 626) not adopted (centralized VBM signature verification + tabulation; that model is Placer's). METRICS: oneweek_pct from CalVoter 'Ballot Processing' per-county charts, all chart-read off the live page (browser): 2022 = 84.7% (statewide 77.7%), 2024 = 81.9% (statewide 86.1%), 2025 = 70.4% (statewide 93.9%). Reads validated against known Madera/San Mateo values in the same charts. electionnight_pct and days_to_90 left null (no certified-final-denominated election-night report and no per-day canvass totals collected; no substitute denominator used). Verifier should: (1) re-read the chart-read CalVoter percentages precisely, (2) try to pin the ASV software vendor and the first election it was used in (could predate Nov 2020), and (3) if a dedicated tablet e-pollbook is later documented, update the epollbook vendor.
